reasoning: Volatility indices like OVX are mean-reverting and right-skewed. This structural property means they typically drift downwards on more than 50% of trading days, punctuated by occasional sharp upward spikes, making the median change negative over most horizons. Because the recent value of 37.9 is mildly elevated relative to standard historical norms (which typically center around the high 20s to low 30s), there is an increased likelihood of mean reversion downwards unless sustained new shocks occur. Lacking dynamic search tools to evaluate real-time geopolitical oil shocks, I rely on the baseline statistical drift. The probability of an increase is conservatively set below 0.5 (near the empirical FRED question base rate of 0.422) and gradually slopes toward 0.5 as the horizon increases and uncertainty widens.
